input.command:
kubectl get namespaces
echo "=== spark-etl pods ==="
kubectl get pods -n spark-etl -o wide
echo "=== jobs ==="
kubectl get jobs -n spark-etl

content.text: Job is currently running. Let me collect the failure information as it happens.

input.command:
NS=spark-etl
echo "=== current etl-job logs ===" && kubectl logs -n $NS -l app=etl-job --tail=100
echo "=== data skew info ===" && kubectl exec -n $NS -l app=data-generator -- cat /data/skew_info.txt 2>&1
echo "=== diagnostic-server logs ===" && kubectl logs -n $NS -l app=diagnostic-server --tail=50

input.command:
NS=spark-etl
POD=$(kubectl get pods -n $NS -l app=data-generator -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}')
kubectl exec -n $NS $POD -- cat /data/skew_info.txt

content.content:
DATA SKEW REPORT
================
total_records=100000
distinct_user_ids=1000
hot_user_id=999
hot_user_records=50000
skew_ratio=500x
note: user_id 999 holds ~50% of all records (Stage 3 aggregation OOMs)

tool_use_result.stdout:
DATA SKEW REPORT
================
total_records=100000
distinct_user_ids=1000
hot_user_id=999
hot_user_records=50000
skew_ratio=500x
note: user_id 999 holds ~50% of all records (Stage 3 aggregation OOMs)

content.text: Now let's wait for the job to fail and capture the exact OOM error.

input.command:
NS=spark-etl
kubectl wait --for=condition=failed job/etl-job -n $NS --timeout=120s 2>&1 || true
echo "=== job status ===" && kubectl get job etl-job -n $NS
echo "=== pods ===" && kubectl get pods -n $NS
